name: Curried Chicken Wings (Ga Lei Gai Yik)
recipeIngredient: 2 Green onions stalks, cut into 1 inch lengths
recipeInstructions:
PREPARATION: Cut off chicken wing tips and save for stock. Cut wings at the joint.
Peel and cut potatoes into chunks and slice onion into small wedges.
COOKING: In a heavy-bottomed 3-quart sauce pot, heat 2 tablespoons of oil and brown ginger,
garlic, and chicken wings for 4 minutes. Add the remaining ingredients except green onions.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 1/2 hour on low heat. Add the green onions during the last 5 minutes.
DO AHEAD NOTES: Cook early in the day. Chicken and potatoes taste better after several hours
in the sauce. It's great the next day.
COMMENTS: Some people are quite surprised that the Chinese use potatoes in their cooking.
It is quite a common vegetable in southern China, although not served the same way as the westerners do.
Curry powder is the most popular seasoning for potatoes. Another option is 5 spice powder.
